items.subjective_preview: Patient presents for 24-week prenatal visit. Feeling well overall. Reports active fetal movement throughout the day. No vaginal bleeding or leakage of...
items.objective_preview: Vitals: BP 118/72 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, Temp 98.4F, Wt 162 lbs (pre-pregnancy 148 lbs, weight gain 14 lbs). Fundal height: 24 cm - appropriate for gestatio...
items.assessment:
1. Intrauterine pregnancy at 24 weeks - progressing normally
2. Carpal tunnel syndrome of pregnancy - bilateral, mild
items.plan:
1. Anatomy ultrasound ordered for detailed fetal survey
2. Wrist splints for carpal tunnel, especially nighttime use
3. Glucose challenge test (1-hour GCT) at 28-week visit
4. Continue prenatal vitamins and iron supplementation
5. Kick count education provided - count to 10 movements, should take <2 hours
6. Return in 4 weeks for 28-week visit
